title: Politics - The Kathmandu Post
authors: By Ganga BC & Jaya Singh Mahara
summary: Politics In candid discussion, chief ministers urge PM to empower provinces By Rajesh Mishra Provincial leaders cite gaps in laws, fiscal transfers and policing authority, as prime minister pledges to strengthen federalism. Congress, UML must reform provincial governance for their own good, expert says By Rajesh Mishra The RSP-led federal government’s reform drive puts pressure on traditional part
content:
Politics In candid discussion, chief ministers urge PM to empower provinces By Rajesh Mishra Provincial leaders cite gaps in laws, fiscal transfers and policing authority, as prime minister pledges to strengthen federalism. Congress, UML must reform provincial governance for their own good, expert says By Rajesh Mishra The RSP-led federal government’s reform drive puts pressure on traditional parties to trim provincial cabinets. Former President Bhandari eyes active political role as dissidents seek to reorganise UML By Purushottam Poudel Last year, Bhandari could not muster enough strength to beat Oli, who was sitting prime minister. Oli’s wings have been clipped now. Government yet to move on by-elections despite 30 provincial and local seats vacant By Rajesh Mishra Constitutional provisions mandate polls to fill provincial and local vacancies, but no consultations have begun as the Election Commission awaits government initiative. Nepal and India to prioritise agendas before high-level visits By Anil Giri India will send a panel led by Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri after Nepal clarifies its priorities. Prime Minister Shah recommends Ramjee Yadav and Gauri Kumari Yadav as ministers By Jaya Singh Mahara Labour and industry portfolios filled after vacancy and reshuffle. Constitution amendment task force holds first meeting today By Ganga BC Panel begins work on amendment discussion paper; Congress and other parties yet to name members. RSP’s rise clouds future of mid-level reformers in old parties By Purushottam Poudel But analysts say capable reform-minded leaders can still rise if they adapt. DP Aryal elected Speaker unopposed By Post Report First non-communist Speaker in 18 years takes office as parties turn to deputy Speaker race. Ruling RSP is weighing which party will get its woman parliamentarian elected deputy Speaker By Purushottam Poudel Aryal was uncontested for Speaker but there are several opposition parties staking their claim to the crucial House role. New House session begins amid blame game By Post Report First sitting endorses interim rules as UML objects and Congress questions probe into Gen Z unrest. Shah consults lawmakers on governance and budget priorities By Ganga BC & Jaya Singh Mahara Prime Minister Balendra Shah meets MPs from three provinces as he builds a national plan focused on reforms, budget allocation and development. Ruling Rastriya Swatantra Party to deploy lawmakers, alongside ministers, to address people’s concerns By Anil Giri Party spokesman Jha says the party is sending five members of parliament to each ministry. Old guard repeats old mistakes even after new political party runs over them By Purushottam Poudel March 5 polls punished traditional parties, who ruled the country for decades. Local and provincial elections are due next year. Planned ban on student unions sparks debate on fundamental right By Purushottam Poudel The government aims to dismantle all political student unions in educational institutions within 60 days. Congress factions parallel activities rattle party ranks By Post Report Dissident camp hosts lawmakers, uses official party letterhead. Thapa camp mulls disciplinary action. Can the government deliver on its ambitious 100-point roadmap? By Purushottam Poudel In the work plan unveiled on Saturday, the Balendra Shah-led Cabinet aims to implement time-bound reforms, enhance service delivery, and address public grievances. Court remands Oli, Lekhak to five days in custody By Post Report Former prime minister and ex-home minister face investigation over deaths during Gen Z protests. NCP picks coordinator Dahal as parliamentary party leader By Post Report As parliamentary party leader, Dahal will now appoint the deputy leader and the chief whip. UML seeks to build joint front against Oli, Lekhak arrests By Post Report Both Congress camps hold meetings to discuss the scenario following leaders’ arrests. Cadres alerted to brace up for ‘any situation’.

Ruling RSP is weighing which party will get its woman parliamentarian elected deputy Speaker By Purushottam Poudel Aryal was uncontested for Speaker but there are several opposition parties staking their claim to the crucial House role. Local and provincial elections are due next year. Planned ban on student unions sparks debate on fundamental right By Purushottam Poudel The government aims to dismantle all political student unions in educational institutions within 60 days.
